199	- **Do NOT use undefined macros.** Every `\foo` your prose needs must exist in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex` or be `\providecommand`'d at the top of your chapter (chapter-local is fine; report a "needs macro" note for shared ones).
200	- **Do NOT touch protected blocks.** Your invocation prompt's "Protected by the mathematician" section lists `archon-protected.yaml` rules. A `statement`-protected label's declaration block (statement + `\label`/`\lean`/`\uses`) is frozen — you may still write its `\begin{proof}`; an `all`-protected label is entirely off-limits. You may freely `\uses{}` protected labels from other blocks. If your directive conflicts with a protection rule, stop and report the conflict instead of editing.
201	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
202	- **Do NOT fabricate citations.** Never write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from memory. If you don't have a local `references/<file>.md` containing the source text, dispatch a retriever and wait. The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical is the discipline check — if you cannot truthfully point to the file you read, the citation block is not allowed in the chapter.
203	- **Do NOT translate or restate the verbatim quote.** `% SOURCE QUOTE:` and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` contain the source's original language, original notation, every word as-is. Project-notation rewrites belong in the rendered prose body, not in the verbatim comment.

205	## Mathlib dependency anchors (`\mathlibok`)
206	
207	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:
208	
209	- makes the reliance explicit and readable (a mathematician sees exactly which Mathlib result the route stands on);
210	- gives `leandag` a real node, so the `\uses{}` resolves (no broken/unknown ref) and the node's effort is **done** rather than ∞;
211	- keeps it clear how much the route leans on Mathlib vs. on original work.
212	
213	Write the anchor like any statement block, with three differences:
214	
215	```latex
216	\begin{lemma}[Hensel's lemma]
217	  \label{lem:hensel_mathlib}
218	  \lean{Mathlib.RingTheory.Henselian.foo}   % the REAL Mathlib declaration
219	  \mathlibok
220	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
221	  Statement of the result in the project's notation.
222	\end{lemma}
223	```
224	
225	- **`\lean{}` names the actual Mathlib declaration** (with its real namespace), so `leandag` matches the node to Mathlib.
226	- **`\mathlibok`** marks it as supplied by Mathlib — the DAG treats it as done; no `\begin{proof}` is needed.
227	- No `% SOURCE:`/`% SOURCE QUOTE:` block — Mathlib is the source; the `\lean{}` target *is* the citation.
228	
229	**The anti-hallucination rule (mirrors citation discipline):** mark `\mathlibok` ONLY when the statement genuinely exists in Mathlib and your stated form is faithful to it. A wrong or invented `\mathlibok` is worse than an ∞ hole — the loop will treat a non-existent result as available and skip proving a real gap. So:
230	
231	- Prefer anchors the **directive names** (the plan agent/reviewer identified them as Mathlib results).
232	- If you are not confident the exact statement is in Mathlib, do **not** mark `\mathlibok` — write the block without it (or leave the `\uses{}` for the reviewer to resolve) and flag it under "Notes for Plan Agent" so the reviewer can verify against Lean.
233	- Never put `\mathlibok` on one of the project's **own** to-be-proved declarations. That marker is for Mathlib-supplied results only; `\leanok` (project proofs) is never yours to add.

3	## Mode

11	
12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	We need an isomorphism of `ModuleCat R'` objects realising a ring base-change /
14	restriction-extension square (`(A⊗_R R')⊗_A M ≅ R'⊗_R M`), built so that the `R'`-action on
15	the carriers is *usable by typeclass resolution* — i.e. `smul_zero`/`smul_add` fire — rather
16	than being an opaque `_aux` `Module` def produced by Mathlib's `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars` /
17	`RestrictScalars` functor objects. The question is the canonical Mathlib idiom for stating and
18	consuming such a square iso transparently.
19	
20	## Empirical root cause (confirmed this iter, not merely suspected)
21	At the `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (e := { g with map_smul' := ?_ })` site of
22	`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` (`FlatBaseChange.lean:909`), the `R'`-module instances on the
23	carriers are the opaque
24	`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv._aux_3 / ._aux_5 : Module ↑R' ↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars …).obj M)`.
25	The two `r' • 0 = 0` zero branches of the `TensorProduct.induction_on` proof of `map_smul'`
26	**cannot close by any of** `simp only [smul_zero, map_zero]` ("simp made no progress"),
27	`rw [smul_zero]` ("did not find pattern `?a • 0`"), or `erw [smul_zero]`
28	("**failed to synthesize `SMulZeroClass ↑R' ↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars …).obj M)`**").
29	The headline is the last: `SMulZeroClass R'` does not synthesize *from* the opaque `_aux_3`
30	`Module R'` instance, so the `smul_zero` lemma cannot even be stated against the goal, let
31	alone rewritten. By contrast, the `add`/`tmul` branches close by `erw [smul_add]` /
32	`erw [ExtendScalars.smul_tmul]` because those lemmas unify against `r' • (a+b)` / `r' • (a⊗s)`
33	subterms already present in the goal (whose `SMul` instance is fixed by the term), needing no
34	fresh class synthesis. The asymmetry is intrinsic: `smul_zero`'s `0` carries no `SMul` subterm
35	to pin the instance, so the elaborator falls back to synthesising `SMulZeroClass` and dies on
36	the opaque `_aux`. `letI`/`inferInstanceAs`/`.toDistribMulAction` re-supply a *fresh* `_aux`
37	that TC still ignores or that `whnf`-times-out. **The in-place `map_smul'`-over-functor-object
38	route is therefore structurally dead, not merely hard.**
39	
40	Note also: the optimistic note at `FlatBaseChange.lean:851` ("`exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
41	(base_change_regroup_linearEquiv ↑M)` typechecks once the helper is in a separate compiled
42	module") is **stale/wrong**; the detailed note at `:911` is **correct**. Tested directly: the
43	imported helper has type `(A⊗[R]R')⊗[A]M ≃ₗ[R'] R'⊗[R]M` with the *canonical* `Module A (A⊗R')`
44	(`Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`) and `Module R M`, and `exact … .toModuleIso` fails with
45	"Application type mismatch … `(A⊗[R]R')⊗[A]M ≃ₗ[R'] …` but expected
46	`↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars includeLeftRingHom).obj M) ≃ₗ[R'] …`". The `⊗[A]` carriers differ
47	by the `Module A (A⊗R')` diamond (`includeLeftRingHom.toAlgebra` as used by `extendScalars`
48	vs `leftAlgebra` as used by `cancelBaseChange`); unifying them forces `whnf` of the opaque
49	object instance and does not go through.
50	
51	## Failed approaches (from directive)
52	- Hand-built `g : LinearEquiv` over the bare functor-object carriers, `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso` →
53	  `map_smul'` zero branches blocked on opaque `Module ↑R'` (confirmed above).
54	- `erw [ExtendScalars.smul_tmul]` + `show` reductions → close `tmul`/`add`, leave the two `zero`
55	  branches with no tensor head to rewrite and no `SMulZeroClass` to synthesize.
56	- `letI`/`haveI`/`inferInstanceAs` re-supplying `SMulZeroClass`/`DistribMulAction` → fresh opaque
57	  `_aux`, ignored by TC; `.toDistribMulAction` projection `whnf`-times-out.
58	
59	## Analogues found (ranked by porting cost, lowest first)
60	
61	### Analogue: `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`)
62	- **Domain**: commutative algebra / ring base change (pushout of `CommRing`s).
63	- **Same structural problem there**: the *pushout-form* base-change cancellation. With
64	  `[Algebra.IsPushout R S A B]`,
65	  `cancelBaseChange R S A B M : B ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[S] S ⊗[R] M`.
66	  Instantiated at `R=R, S=R', A=A, B=A⊗[R]R', M=M` it is **exactly the regroup equiv**
67	  `(A⊗[R]R') ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[R'] R' ⊗[R] M`, and the `Algebra.IsPushout R R' A (A⊗[R]R')` instance
68	  it needs is supplied for free by `TensorProduct.isPushout'` (`Algebra.IsPushout R T S (R⊗S T)`
69	  with `S=A, T=R'`). Companions: `cancelBaseChange_tmul`, `cancelBaseChange_symm_tmul`,
70	  `toLinearEquiv_cancelBaseChangeAlg`.
71	- **Technique**: the equivalence is **natively `≃ₗ[S] = ≃ₗ[R']`** — Mathlib already discharges
72	  `R'`-linearity inside the bundled `LinearEquiv`. There is **no hand-written `map_smul'`**, hence
73	  **no `TensorProduct.induction_on`, hence no `r' • 0 = 0` zero branch at all**. This is the
74	  decisive structural improvement over the project's current core
75	  `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R A A M R'`, which is only `≃ₗ[A]` (its `B`
76	  slot is taken as `A`), forcing the project to *re-bundle* `R'`-linearity by hand via
77	  `rightAlgebra` + `comm` + a manual `map_smul'` — the exact source of the dead zero branches.
78	- **Mapping to project**: replace the `RegroupHelper.lean` core
79	  `base_change_regroup_linearEquiv` (`comm ≪≫ ATM.cancelBaseChange ≪≫ comm` + hand `map_smul'`)
80	  by `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange ↑R ↑R' ↑A (↑A ⊗[↑R] ↑R') ↑M` (or keep the helper but
81	  swap its body). Then `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` is `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso` of it, modulo
82	  the **one** residual obstruction shared by every approach: the `Module A (A⊗R')` diamond between
83	  `cancelBaseChange`'s canonical carrier and `extendScalars`'s functor-object carrier.
84	- **Porting cost**: low-to-medium. The R'-linearity problem evaporates; the only remaining work is
85	  the A-diamond carrier bridge (see "How to resolve the residual diamond" below), which is an
86	  *object-level* `≃ₗ`/`eqToIso` reconciliation that never writes an element-level `r' • 0`.
87	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
88	
89	### Analogue: `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` / `restrictScalarsId` / `extendScalarsComp` / `extendScalarsId` / `restrictScalarsCongr` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings`)
90	- **Domain**: category theory of change-of-rings functors.
91	- **Same structural problem there**: building a change-of-rings *tower* iso transparently. The key
92	  fact is that `restrictScalarsComp'App_hom_apply` / `_inv_apply` are **`= x` (the identity on
93	  the carrier set)** — the iso relabels the `Module` structure while fixing the underlying
94	  element. Hence an iso assembled from these never produces an element-level `r' • 0` goal: all
95	  module-action coherence is internal to the bundled `Iso`.
96	- **Technique**: assemble the comparison as a composite of `Iso`s in `ModuleCat R'`
97	  (`restrictScalarsComp'App` ×n, `extendScalarsId`/`Comp`, `restrictScalarsCongr` from a ring
98	  equation, `eqToIso`, `Functor.mapIso` of a smaller iso) — **never** `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso`
99	  of a hand `AddEquiv` + `map_smul'`. The project's own `gammaPushforwardIso`
100	  (`FlatBaseChange.lean:288`) is the existence proof that this route closes **axiom-clean** for a
101	  restrict-tower (`restrictScalarsComp'App` ×2 + `restrictScalarsCongr` + `eqToIso` from
102	  `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop`).
103	- **Mapping to project**: use this family to (i) build the A-diamond bridge as an object-level
104	  iso, and (ii) if/when the square is restated functorially, glue the extend/restrict pieces.
105	  Caveat: a base-change *square* (Beck–Chevalley) mixes `extend` and `restrict`, so these tower
106	  isos alone do **not** produce the square — `cancelBaseChange` (analogue 1) supplies the genuine
107	  associativity content; this family supplies the transparent *packaging*.
108	- **Porting cost**: low (already in the project's toolbox; `gammaPushforwardIso` is the template).
109	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
110	
111	### Analogue: `CommRingCat.moduleCatRestrictScalarsPseudofunctor` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Pseudofunctor`) and `CategoryTheory.TwoSquare` / Guitart-exactness (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.GuitartExact.KanExtension`, `isIso_lanBaseChange_app_iff`)
112	- **Domain**: 2-category theory / pseudofunctors / mate-of-a-square (Beck–Chevalley) calculus.
113	- **Same structural problem there**: restriction of scalars as a *pseudofunctor*
114	  `CommRingCatᵒᵖ → Cat` (`mapId = restrictScalarsId`, `mapComp = restrictScalarsComp`) — the
115	  canonical "coherent gluing of module categories across ring maps" — and the abstract
116	  base-change *square* as a `TwoSquare` whose mate is invertible iff the square is Guitart-exact.
117	- **Technique**: phrase the square iso as a 2-cell / mate in the pseudofunctor, so coherence is
118	  inherited from the (already proven) pseudofunctor/`TwoSquare` laws rather than recomputed.
119	- **Mapping to project**: this is the "right" long-run home for a reusable `ModuleCat`-level
120	  Beck–Chevalley square iso (which Mathlib does **not** currently ship for restrict∘extend — a
121	  genuine upstream gap). For *this* obligation it is over-engineered; cited as the principled
122	  target if the square iso is ever needed in more generality.
123	- **Porting cost**: high (would build a `TwoSquare`/pseudofunctor-mate layer).
124	- **Verdict**: PARTIAL_ANALOGUE.
125	
126	## How to resolve the residual `Module A (A⊗R')` diamond (shared by all routes)
127	The diamond is `includeLeftRingHom.toAlgebra` (used by `ModuleCat.extendScalars includeLeftRingHom`)
128	vs `Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra` (used by `cancelBaseChange`). They are equal-as-data but
129	distinct instance terms, so the two `⊗[A]` carrier *types* differ. Critically, with analogue 1 the
130	diamond is the **only** thing left, and it lives at the *object/carrier* level, where it can be
131	discharged **without** any element-level `r' • 0`:
132	- as an `≃ₗ[R']` carrier-swap that is `id` on elements with `map_smul' := fun _ _ => rfl`
133	  (both sides' `R'`-action are `restrictScalars includeRight` definitionally — the prover's
134	  existing `eT` is the `A`-linear version of this; the `R'`-linear version composes directly with
135	  the Mathlib-proven `cancelBaseChange` and needs no induction), or
136	- absorbed into the `moduleSpecΓFunctor.mapIso`/tilde-dictionary chains the consumers
137	  (`base_change_mate_codomain_read`, `…domain_read`) already build, via the
138	  `restrictScalarsComp'App` family (analogue 2).
139	The point is that the **only** place `R'`-linearity is computed is now inside Mathlib's
140	`Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`; the project writes at most a `rfl`-`map_smul'` identity
141	bridge, never a `TensorProduct.induction_on` over the opaque carrier.
142	
143	## Verdict on the directive's two routes
144	- Directive **(a)** — "retype `g`'s domain/codomain at genuine restrict/extend objects so the
145	  action is transparent (prover's route (b))": **RECOMMENDED, in the refined form above.**
146	  Retyping at the *same* functor objects does NOT help (they ARE the opaque carriers — confirmed:
147	  the goal at `:909` already is between functor objects). What helps is switching the *core* to the
148	  natively-`≃ₗ[R']` `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`, eliminating the hand `map_smul'`
149	  entirely, and resolving the A-diamond at the object level.
150	- Directive **(b)** — "abandon the mate equiv, reduce both sides through the tilde dictionaries to
151	  `cancelBaseChange` directly": **NOT RECOMMENDED as a primary.** The consumers
152	  `base_change_mate_domain_read`/`codomain_read` are *typed at the functor objects*, so the same
153	  A-diamond bridge is unavoidable whether or not a named `regroupEquiv` exists — option (b) inlines
154	  the identical obstruction into `base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq` while discarding the clean
155	  blueprint-referenced named declaration. Moreover `generator_trace_eq`/`_trace` are `sorry` for an
156	  *unrelated* reason (the adjoint-mate unwinding of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap`), which option (b)
157	  does not advance. Keep the named `regroupEquiv`; fix its construction per (a-refined).
158	
159	## Top suggestion
160	Swap the regroup core to `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange ↑R ↑R' ↑A (↑A ⊗[↑R] ↑R') ↑M`
161	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`; `IsPushout` from `TensorProduct.isPushout'`), which is
162	natively `≃ₗ[R']` and so carries no hand `map_smul'` and no `r' • 0` zero branch. Promote it with
163	`LinearEquiv.toModuleIso`, reconciling the lone `Module A (A⊗R')` diamond against

165	bridge (the `R'`-linear analogue of the existing `eT`, `map_smul' := fun _ _ => rfl`), modelled on

2	\label{chap:Cohomology_RegroupHelper}
3	% This chapter blueprints the standalone, geometry-free helper that lives in
4	% AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/RegroupHelper.lean (1:1 slug). It is the pure
5	% tensor-algebra core of lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv in the FlatBaseChange
6	% chapter; it is split into its own compilation unit so the object-level
7	% ModuleCat iso can be obtained by a single LinearEquiv.toModuleIso application
8	% (the separate import normalises the `Module A (A ⊗[R] R')` instance diamond
9	% that blocks the same-file construction).
10	
11	\section{The R'-linear regrouping equivalence}
12	
13	\begin{lemma}
14	
15	\leanok
16	[Regrouping linear equivalence for the tensor tower]
17	  \label{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
18	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
19	  \uses{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
20	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
21	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
22	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
23	  % L933--938).
24	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
25	  % equality
26	  % $$
27	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
28	  % $$
29	  % as $R'$-modules."
30	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
31	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
32	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\)
33	  an \(A\)-module. On the \emph{canonical} Mathlib instances (with \(R'\) acting on
34	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) through the \(\texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra}\)
35	  factor, i.e.\ \(r' \cdot (a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\)) there is a bundled
36	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
37	  \[
38	    \operatorname{regroup} :
39	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
40	  \]
41	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the affine base-change source. On generators
42	  it sends \((a \otimes r') \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes (a \cdot m)\); in
43	  particular \((1 \otimes r') \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\), with inverse
44	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
45	  hypothesis.
46	\end{lemma}
47	
48	\begin{proof}
49	  \uses{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
50	  \leanok
51	  This is pure tensor algebra, assembled from Mathlib's heterobasic equivalences
52	  with no geometry:
53	  \[
54	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M
55	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \texttt{comm}\ }\; M \otimes_A (A \otimes_R R')
56	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\ }\; M \otimes_R R'
57	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \texttt{comm}\ }\; R' \otimes_R M .
58	  \]
59	  The first and last arrows are the \(R'\)-linear heterobasic commutativity
60	  isomorphisms \(\texttt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.comm}\) (the \(R'\)-action
61	  enters through the \(A \otimes_R R'\) factor, so the \(R'\)-linear versions apply);
62	  the middle arrow is \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) of
63	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}, instantiated at the tower
64	  \(R \to A \to A\) with module \(M\) and base-change object \(R'\). On the generator
65	  \[
66	    (a \otimes r') \otimes m \;\mapsto\; m \otimes (a \otimes r')
67	    \;\xmapsto{\ \operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\ }\; (a \cdot m) \otimes r'
68	    \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m) ,
69	  \]
70	  using \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}(m \otimes_A (a \otimes_R r')) = (a \cdot m)
71	  \otimes_R r'\) (\(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul}\)). The \(R'\)-linearity of the
72	  composite is checked on generators by \(\texttt{TensorProduct.induction\_on}\):
73	  the \(\texttt{rightAlgebra}\) action \(r' \cdot (a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\)
74	  combines with \(\texttt{comm\_tmul}\) and \(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul}\) to
75	  transport the scalar through each factor. Being a composite of linear equivalences
76	  it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of the three factors carries a
77	  flatness hypothesis.
78	\end{proof}

947	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change]
948	\label{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}
949	Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
950	$$
951	\xymatrix{
952	X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
953	S' \ar[r]^g & S
954	}
955	$$
956	Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
957	with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
958	Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
959	For any $i \geq 0$
960	\begin{enumerate}
961	\item the base change map of
962	Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
963	is an isomorphism
964	$$
965	g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
966	$$
967	\item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
968	$H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
969	\end{enumerate}
970	\end{lemma}
971	
972	\begin{proof}
973	Using Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case} in (1)
974	is allowed since $g'$ is flat by
975	Morphisms, Lemma \ref{morphisms-lemma-base-change-flat}.
976	Having said this, part (1) follows from part (2). Namely,
977	part (1) is local on $S'$ and hence we may assume $S$
978	and $S'$ are affine. In other words, we have $S = \Spec(A)$
979	and $S' = \Spec(B)$ as in (2).
980	Then since $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is quasi-coherent
981	(Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}),
982	it is the quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_S$-module associated to the

912	a quasi-coherent sheaf, and for every base change diagram
913	(\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})

915	$$

925	$S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$

1244	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1245	  % $$
1246	  % as $R'$-modules."

1248	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1249	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1250	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1251	  \[
1252	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1253	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1254	  \]
1255	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1256	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1257	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1258	  hypothesis.
1259	\end{lemma}
1260	
1261	\begin{proof}
1262	  \uses{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib, lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
1263	  The underlying additive bijection is supplied by the standalone, geometry-free
1264	  \(R'\)-linear equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}; the work
1265	  of this proof is to repackage that equivalence as a morphism of \(R'\)-module objects.
1266	  Two points of bookkeeping make the repackaging non-trivial, and pinning them down
1267	  \emph{is} the content of the proof. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1268	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1269	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1270	
1271	  \emph{The two \(A\)-actions are distinct types, so an identity bridge is mandatory.}
1272	  The source of the standalone equivalence, \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), is the
1273	  relative tensor product over the \emph{canonical} \(A\)-action on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1274	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the
1275	  carrier object produced by the dictionaries is
1276	  \((\operatorname{extendScalars}\iota)\,M\) with \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the
1277	  left inclusion, whose relative tensor product is taken over the
1278	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\iota\) \(A\)-action. These two \(A\)-module structures on
1279	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) coincide on elements, but the tensor product depends on the
1280	  \(A\)-module structure in its type, so the two tensor products are \emph{not equal}
1281	  even though their underlying abelian groups are the same. One therefore inserts the identity \(A\)-linear bridge
1282	  \[
1283	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M
1284	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1285	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M ,
1286	  \]
1287	  which re-reads the same generators against the carrier's \(A\)-action. This bridge is
1288	  the mathematically essential glue between the standalone helper and the object; it
1289	  cannot be elided.
1290	
1291	  \emph{The residue is the \(R'\)-linearity of the assembled additive map, checked on
1292	  generators.} After the bridge, the only remaining obligation is that the assembled
1293	  additive equivalence respects the \(R'\)-module structures. The \(R'\)-action on the
1294	  carrier is restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1295	  \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\), so \(r' \cdot y = (1 \otimes r') \cdot y\). On a
1296	  generator the extension-of-scalars action then computes
1297	  \[
1298	    (1 \otimes r') \cdot \bigl((a \otimes s) \otimes m\bigr)
1299	    = \bigl((1 \otimes r')(a \otimes s)\bigr) \otimes m
1300	    = \bigl(a \otimes (r' s)\bigr) \otimes m ,
1301	  \]
1302	  using \((1 \otimes r')(a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\) in \(A \otimes_R R'\). The
1303	  helper's underlying map sends \((a \otimes s) \otimes m \mapsto s \otimes (a \cdot m)\)
1304	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}), so it carries
1305	  \(\bigl(a \otimes (r' s)\bigr) \otimes m\) to
1306	  \((r' s) \otimes (a \cdot m) = r' \cdot \bigl(s \otimes (a \cdot m)\bigr)\), which is
1307	  \(r'\) times the image of the original generator. Thus the additive map commutes with
1308	  the \(R'\)-action on generators, hence everywhere by bilinearity; together with the
1309	  bridge \(eT\) this promotes the standalone equivalence to the desired bundled
1310	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism. Being a composite of linear equivalences the result is an
1311	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents carries a flatness hypothesis.
1312	\end{proof}

\subsection{The section-level base-change identity}

The section-level identification
Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} below is the single
highest-leverage obligation of the affine close, and we prove it \emph{directly on
global sections}, with no abstract adjoint-mate tower. In the affine--affine model the
base-change map \(\theta\) is, after its domain and codomain are read through the two
proved tilde dictionaries, exactly the \(R'\)-base change of the algebraic unit of
extension of scalars; post-composing with the canonical regrouping isomorphism
\((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \cong R' \otimes_R M\) identifies it with the canonical
base-change comparison, an isomorphism carrying no flatness hypothesis. The computation
factors through three named reads — the domain read, the codomain read, and the single
\emph{section-level identity} that the conjugated map is the base change of the unit —
together with the one Mathlib-absent crux identifying the pullback cone legs with the
\(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of the tensor inclusions
(Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}). Two Mathlib results are recorded
first as dependency anchors.

\begin{proof}

  The underlying additive bijection is supplied by the standalone, geometry-free
  \(R'\)-linear equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}; the work
  of this proof is to repackage that equivalence as a morphism of \(R'\)-module objects.
  Two points of bookkeeping make the repackaging non-trivial, and pinning them down
  \emph{is} the content of the proof. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
  per the tensor-order convention note).

  \emph{The two \(A\)-actions are distinct types, so an identity bridge is mandatory.}
  The source of the standalone equivalence, \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), is the
  relative tensor product over the \emph{canonical} \(A\)-action on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the
  carrier object produced by the dictionaries is
  \((\operatorname{extendScalars}\iota)\,M\) with \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the
  left inclusion, whose relative tensor product is taken over the
  \(\operatorname{restr}_\iota\) \(A\)-action. These two \(A\)-module structures on
  \(A \otimes_R R'\) coincide on elements, but the tensor product depends on the
  \(A\)-module structure in its type, so the two tensor products are \emph{not equal}
  even though their underlying abelian groups are the same. One therefore inserts the identity \(A\)-linear bridge
  \[
    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M
    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M ,
  \]
  which re-reads the same generators against the carrier's \(A\)-action. This bridge is
  the mathematically essential glue between the standalone helper and the object; it
  cannot be elided.

  \emph{The residue is the \(R'\)-linearity of the assembled additive map, checked on
  generators.} After the bridge, the only remaining obligation is that the assembled
  additive equivalence respects the \(R'\)-module structures. The \(R'\)-action on the
  carrier is restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\),
  \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\), so \(r' \cdot y = (1 \otimes r') \cdot y\). On a
  generator the extension-of-scalars action then computes
  \[
    (1 \otimes r') \cdot \bigl((a \otimes s) \otimes m\bigr)
    = \bigl((1 \otimes r')(a \otimes s)\bigr) \otimes m
    = \bigl(a \otimes (r' s)\bigr) \otimes m ,
  \]
  using \((1 \otimes r')(a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\) in \(A \otimes_R R'\). The
  helper's underlying map sends \((a \otimes s) \otimes m \mapsto s \otimes (a \cdot m)\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}), so it carries
  \(\bigl(a \otimes (r' s)\bigr) \otimes m\) to
  \((r' s) \otimes (a \cdot m) = r' \cdot \bigl(s \otimes (a \cdot m)\bigr)\), which is
  \(r'\) times the image of the original generator. Thus the additive map commutes with
  the \(R'\)-action on generators, hence everywhere by bilinearity; together with the
  bridge \(eT\) this promotes the standalone equivalence to the desired bundled
  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism. Being a composite of linear equivalences the result is an
  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents carries a flatness hypothesis.
\end{proof}

  \(1 \otimes m\). Under the pullback dictionary
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, naturality at the unit identifies
  \((g')^*\widetilde M\) on global sections with the extension of scalars
  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) along \(A \to R' \otimes_R A\) and carries the unit
  element \(1 \otimes m\) to \((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\). Composing with the codomain
  read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}
  leaves the value \((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) on the generator \(m\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Pseudofunctor reindexing of the pushforward target]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}
  In the affine--affine model, applying \(f_* = \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\) to the
  unit assignment \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value} leaves it unchanged on elements
  (restriction of scalars is the identity on underlying sets), and the pseudofunctor
  identities \((g'f)_* = (f'g)_* = g_*\,f'_*\) (forced by the commutativity
  \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) of the square) reindex the target as the \(R'\)-module
  \(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M = (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) of the codomain read
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}
  Restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\) does not move elements, so \(f_*\) carries
  the assignment \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) to itself on underlying sets.
  The coherence of the pushforward pseudofunctor under the equality
  \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) — composition of pushforwards together with congruence
  under equal morphisms — rewrites \((g'f)_*\widetilde M\) as
  \(g_*\,f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\), whose inner object
  \(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\) is read by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} as \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\).
  This reindexing is exactly the in-file pushforward-composition and
  pushforward-congruence naturality applied to the codomain dictionary; no new content
  beyond those category-level identities is needed.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Transpose-on-elements formula for the base-change adjunction]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}
  % NOTE: the (g^* ⊣ g_*) adjunction here is the affine base-change /
  % extension-restriction adjunction along ψ : R → R'; the formula below is the
  % standard transpose-on-elements identity for an extension-of-scalars adjunction,
  % so it stands on its proof sketch with no external reference.
  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\). For an \(R\)-linear map
  \(u : \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M \to \operatorname{restr}_\psi N\), the
  \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-transpose \(\widehat u\) is the unique \(R'\)-linear map
  \(\widehat u : R' \otimes_R M \to N\) determined on generators by
  \(\widehat u(r' \otimes m) = r' \cdot u(m)\). Applied to the unit assignment
  \(u : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}, with the source read as
  \(R' \otimes_R M\) by Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}, the transpose is
  \[
    r' \otimes m \;\longmapsto\; r' \cdot \bigl((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\bigr)
    \;=\; (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m ,
  \]
  the \(R'\)-action scaling the \(R'\)-tensor factor of \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}
  The transpose under an extension-of-scalars adjunction
  \(g^* = R' \otimes_R (-)\) is the unique \(R'\)-linear extension of \(u\) along the
  unit \(m \mapsto 1 \otimes m\), so it is determined on generators by
  \(\widehat u(r' \otimes m) = r' \cdot u(m)\). Substituting
  \(u(m) = (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) and using that the \(R'\)-action on
  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) scales the \(R'\)-tensor factor
  (\(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\)) yields the stated
  value. The source identification \(R' \otimes_R M\) is the domain read
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Generator identification of the conjugated section-level map]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_generator_trace_eq}
  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex,
        lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
  % L933--938).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
  % equality
  % $$
  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
  % $$
  % as $R'$-modules."
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
  With the domain and codomain pinned by Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}
  and Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}, the conjugated section-level
  base-change map
  \[
    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} :
    R' \otimes_R M \longrightarrow (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M
  \]
  sends \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\); equivalently it equals the
  \emph{inverse} of the regrouping isomorphism
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}, i.e.\
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}
  = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex,
        lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}
  By Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}, \(\alpha\) is the
  \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-transpose of \(f_*(\eta')\), with \(\eta'\) the
  \(\bigl((g')^*, (g')_*\bigr)\)-unit. The computation is the thin assembly of three
  isolated steps, each reading the relevant functor on global modules through the
  dictionaries of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read} and
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
  \begin{itemize}
    \item[(A)] \emph{The unit value.} By
      Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}, the \(\bigl((g')^*,(g')_*\bigr)\)-unit
      \(\eta'\), read through \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\), is the base-change unit
      \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).
    \item[(C)] \emph{Pushforward and reindex.} By
      Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}, applying
      \(f_* = \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\) leaves this assignment unchanged on
      elements and reindexes the target, via the pseudofunctor identities, as the
      codomain read \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\).
    \item[(B)] \emph{Transpose under \((g^* \dashv g_*)\).} By
      Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}, the transpose of the assignment
      \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) is the \(R'\)-linear map
      \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \bigl((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\bigr)
      = (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\), with source read as \(R' \otimes_R M\) by
      Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}.
  \end{itemize}
  Composing steps (A), (C), (B) shows that
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}\) sends
  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). This assignment is exactly the
  inverse generator of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}, so the conjugated
  map agrees with \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\) on generators and hence (both being
  \(R'\)-linear) everywhere. This is exactly the ``boils down to the equality''
  computation of the source.
\end{proof}

% --- Section-level identity (the single direct-on-sections obligation that replaces the
%     former three-step adjoint-mate tower). The base-change map on sections is the
%     R'-base change of the algebraic unit of extension of scalars; reading it through the
%     two tilde dictionaries pins it as the inverse of the regrouping isomorphism. ---

\begin{lemma}
[The section-level base-change map is the base change of the unit]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_section_identity}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_section_identity}
  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
  % L932--937).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
  % equality
  % $$
  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
  % $$
  % as $R'$-modules."
  % SOURCE (cohomological form): [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KH
  % `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology', part (2) (read from
  % references/stacks-coherent.tex, L967--968) — the H^0/direct-image-with-base-change
  % specialisation this affine identity underlies.
  % SOURCE QUOTE (cohomological form): "if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$."
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step); cohomological form Tag 02KH(2).}
  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
  %   The conjugate of `Γ(θ)` through the domain read `Θ_src` and codomain read `Θ_tgt`
  %   is the `R'`-base change of the algebraic unit `η_M : M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`,
  %   `m ↦ (1 ⊗ₜ 1) ⊗ₜ m` (`LinearMap.lTensor R' η_M` post-composed with the `R'`-action),
  %   which on the generator `r' ⊗ m` returns `r' • ((1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m) = (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`; this is
  %   exactly `(base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv`. The formalized conclusion is the
  %   propositional equality of `ModuleCat R'` morphisms:
  %     {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
  %       (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
  %         (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
  %           (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
  %             (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
  %             (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
  %             (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) ≫
  %         (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
  %         = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv
  In the affine--affine model (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
  \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde M\) for an \(A\)-module
  \(M\)), write \(\Gamma(\theta)\) for the section-level incarnation of the base-change
  map \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}). Conjugate it through the two reads:
  the domain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read})
  identifies its source \(\Gamma\bigl(g^*(f_*\widetilde M)\bigr)\) with \(R' \otimes_R M\),
  and the codomain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) identifies its target
  \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\) with \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\). Then
  the conjugated map
  \[
    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\theta) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} :
    R' \otimes_R M \longrightarrow (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M
  \]
  is the \(R'\)-base change of the algebraic unit of extension of scalars along
  \(A \to A \otimes_R R'\), namely \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\),
  \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\): explicitly it is the unique \(R'\)-linear map
  sending \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \eta_M(m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\)
  (i.e.\ \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\,\eta_M\) followed by the \(R'\)-action on
  the target). Equivalently, this conjugated map is the \emph{inverse} of the regrouping
  isomorphism Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv},
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\theta) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}
  = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\), and in particular an isomorphism with \emph{no}
  flatness hypothesis.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
  The argument is carried out \emph{directly on the module of global sections}; no
  abstract adjoint-mate identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By
  hom-extensionality for \(R'\)-linear maps it suffices to check the asserted equality on
  the \(R'\)-module generators \(r' \otimes m\) of the source \(R' \otimes_R M\), and by
  \(R'\)-linearity on the generators \(1 \otimes m\).

  \emph{The base-change map is the base change of the unit.} For a quasi-coherent sheaf
  on an affine scheme the base-change comparison \(\theta\) is, by construction, the
  pullback–pushforward comparison obtained by applying \(g^*\) to the unit of the
  \(\bigl((g')^*, (g')_*\bigr)\)-adjunction; read on global sections through the two tilde
  dictionaries this is precisely the extension of scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of the
  algebraic unit of extension of scalars along \(A \to A \otimes_R R'\). Concretely, the
  unit \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) sends \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1)
  \otimes m\) (the codomain read identifies \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\)
  with \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}),
  and the domain read identifies the source with \(R' \otimes_R M\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}). The base-change map therefore is the
  \(R'\)-linearization \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\,\eta_M\) of \(\eta_M\)
  evaluated through the \(R'\)-action on the target: on the generator \(r' \otimes m\),
  \[
    r' \otimes m \;\longmapsto\; r' \cdot \eta_M(m)
    \;=\; r' \cdot \bigl((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\bigr)
    \;=\; (1 \otimes r') \otimes m ,
  \]
  using that the \(R'\)-action on \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) is restriction of
  scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\).

  \emph{Identification with \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).} The regrouping isomorphism
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} has inverse sending
  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) on generators. This is the same
  assignment as the displayed map above, and both maps are \(R'\)-linear, so they
  coincide. This is exactly the ``boils down to the equality''
  \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M\) of the source, read as an identity of
  \(R'\)-modules; the regrouping isomorphism realizes the equality and the base-change map
  is its inverse. In particular the conjugated map is an isomorphism, with no flatness
  hypothesis used anywhere.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
  \leanok
  This is pure tensor algebra, assembled from Mathlib's heterobasic equivalences
  with no geometry:
  \[
    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M
    \;\xrightarrow{\ \texttt{comm}\ }\; M \otimes_A (A \otimes_R R')
    \;\xrightarrow{\ \operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\ }\; M \otimes_R R'
    \;\xrightarrow{\ \texttt{comm}\ }\; R' \otimes_R M .
  \]
  The first and last arrows are the \(R'\)-linear heterobasic commutativity
  isomorphisms \(\texttt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.comm}\) (the \(R'\)-action
  enters through the \(A \otimes_R R'\) factor, so the \(R'\)-linear versions apply);
  the middle arrow is \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}, instantiated at the tower
  \(R \to A \to A\) with module \(M\) and base-change object \(R'\). On the generator
  \[
    (a \otimes r') \otimes m \;\mapsto\; m \otimes (a \otimes r')
    \;\xmapsto{\ \operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\ }\; (a \cdot m) \otimes r'
    \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m) ,
  \]
  using \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}(m \otimes_A (a \otimes_R r')) = (a \cdot m)
  \otimes_R r'\) (\(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul}\)). The \(R'\)-linearity of the
  composite is checked on generators by \(\texttt{TensorProduct.induction\_on}\):
  the \(\texttt{rightAlgebra}\) action \(r' \cdot (a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\)
  combines with \(\texttt{comm\_tmul}\) and \(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul}\) to
  transport the scalar through each factor. Being a composite of linear equivalences
  it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of the three factors carries a
  flatness hypothesis.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
  \leanok
  This is pure tensor algebra, and it is supplied \emph{directly} by Mathlib's
  pushout-form base-change cancellation
  \(\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}), instantiated at \(S = R'\),
  \(B = A \otimes_R R'\):
  \[
    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} R\,R'\,A\,(A \otimes_R R')\,M :
    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M .
  \]
  The required pushout instance \(\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout} R\,R'\,A\,
  (A \otimes_R R')\) is supplied for free by \(\operatorname{TensorProduct.isPushout'}\).
  The decisive structural point is that this equivalence is \emph{natively}
  \(R'\)-linear: the cancellation is bundled as a \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over
  \(R'\) (its base-ring slot is \(S = R'\)), so the \(R'\)-linearity is part of the
  Mathlib datum and there is \emph{no} hand-written scalar-compatibility obligation, no
  \(\texttt{TensorProduct.induction\_on}\), and no residual \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) branch.
  On the generator it computes, by \(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul}\),
  \[
    (a \otimes r') \otimes m \;\longmapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m) ,
  \]
  with inverse \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\)
  (\(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_symm\_tmul}\)); the \(R'\)-action enters through the
  \(A \otimes_R R'\) factor (\(r' \cdot (a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\)). Being a
  single bundled Mathlib linear equivalence it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and it
  carries no flatness hypothesis.

  This replaces the earlier hand-assembled core
  \(\texttt{comm} \circ \texttt{AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange} \circ \texttt{comm}\),
  whose middle factor is only \(\texttt{≃ₗ[A]}\) (its \(B\)-slot is taken as \(A\)), which
  forced re-bundling the \(R'\)-linearity by hand through a manual scalar check — the
  source of the dead zero branches. The pushout form avoids that entirely.
\end{proof}
